summary: Touching Scarlet>Fire, debut Days Between, but overall low‑energy show
blurb: The set features a moving Scarlet Begonias → Fire On The Mountain pair and the first appearance of Days Between. While Jerry’s vocals are sometimes buried in the mix, the band delivers solid moments like Wave To The Wind and Throwing Stones.
review: February 22, 1993 at Oakland‑Alameda County Coliseum offers a mixed bag. The second set shines with a heartfelt Scarlet Begonias → Fire On The Mountain segue that many fans describe as “touching,” and the debut of the rarely‑heard Days Between adds a historic note. Jerry’s voice rises above the thin mix on highlights such as Wave To The Wind, delivering the line “when all we ever wanted…” with genuine emotion, while Phil’s bass keeps a steady groove that even inspires a little “Phil Zone” dancing. However, the overall energy feels subdued; the first recording notes a thin vocal balance and Bob’s guitar slipping in and out of the mix, making large portions feel “off.” The drums push forward after Saint Of Circumstance, adding to the uneven feel. For listeners seeking the clearest picture of this night, the good‑quality FLAC capture (gd1993-02-22.149126.mtx.tobin.flac16) presents the strongest sound and preserves the emotional peaks without the distracting mix issues of the board source.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Only one recording expresses clear positivity while the other two are mixed; there is no consistent praise or criticism across multiple sources, so sentiment adjustment remains neutral.

best_recording.reason: Good overall sound quality with no major mix issues, captures the emotional highlights (Scarlet>Fire, Days Between, Wave To The Wind) cleanly.

key_highlights: Touching Scarlet Begonias → Fire On The Mountain segue
Debut of Days Between adds historic significance
Emotional vocal performance on Wave To The Wind
Solid groove through Throwing Stones and Not Fade Away

band_performance.Jerry: Vocals are often low in the mix, but his emotional delivery on Wave To The Wind and the Scarlet>Fire segment stands out.
band_performance.Phil: Bass is prominent and sometimes over‑present, providing a solid foundation that fuels the occasional “Phil Zone” vibe.
band_performance.Bob: Guitar presence fluctuates due to mix instability—sometimes forward, other times nearly absent.
band_performance.Mickey: Drums surge forward after Saint Of Circumstance, contributing to an imbalanced soundstage.
band_performance.Billy: No specific commentary; overall drum work is solid but occasionally over‑emphasized in the mix.
